solution: Dihydrogen gas is converted into liquid state by cooling to 20K. This would require expensive insulated tanks. Tanks are metal alloy like <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NaNi</mi><mn>5</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>Ti</mi><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>TiH</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>Mg</mi><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>MgH</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo></mrow></msub></math> etc. are in use for storage of dihydrogen.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The largest single use of dihydrogen is in the synthesis of ammonia (compound of nitrogen) which is used in the manufacture of nitric acid and and nitrogenous fertilizers like urea.

Around 55% of hydrogen around the world would goes to ammonia production.

subject: Chemistry
solution:

C+H2O→CO+H2 (Water gas or Syn gas);

Water gas is also called syn gas because it is used for synthesis of methanol.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Zn+2NaOH→Na2ZnO2+H2↑

In the above reaction, Zinc reacts with Sodium Hydroxide, gives Sodium Zincate with evolving of hydrogen. Here Zn does not react as an acid in the reaction above since the reaction is not an acid-base one, but rather oxidation-reduction

ZnO22- is anhydrous form of ZnOH42-

subject: Chemistry
options: There is extensive intramolecular hydrogen bonding in the condensed phase.
Ice formed by heavy water sinks in normal water.
Water is oxidized to oxygen during photosynthesis
Water can act both as an acid and as a base
solution:
(i) Ice formed by heavy water sinks in normal water due to higher density of D2O than normal water.

(ii) 6 CO 2 + 6 H 2 O → chlorophyll hv C 6 H 12 O 6 +6O 2

(iii) Water can show amphiprotic nature and hence water can act both as an acid a base.

(iv) There is extensive intermolecular hydrogen bonding in the condensed phase instead of intramolecular H - bonding.

solution: While isotopes of an element have the same electronic configuration, their chemical properties can differ slightly due to differences in their atomic mass. Isotopes of an element have the same number of protons but varying numbers of neutrons, which affects the overall mass of the atom. The only difference is in their rates of reactions, mainly due to their different enthalpy of bond dissociation.

subject: Chemistry
options: Atomic hydrogen is produced when <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> molecules at a high temperature are irradiated with UV radiation.

At around <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2000</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mo>,</mo></math> the dissociation of dihydrogen into its atoms is nearly <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>8</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>%</mo></math>

Bond dissociation enthalpy of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is highest among diatomic gaseous molecules which contain a single bond.

Dihydrogen is produced on reacting zinc with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> as well as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NaOH</mi><mrow><mo>(</mo><mi>aq</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>.</mo></mrow></msub></math>.
solution: Atomic hydrogen is produced at high temperature in an electric arc or under ultraviolet radiations. The dissociation of dihydrogen at 2000 K is only 0.081%. H–H bond dissociation enthalpy is highest for a single bond for any diatomic molecule. Dihydrogen can be produced on reacting Zn with dil. HCl as well as NaOH (aq.).

In SnH4, the central atom tin is sp3 hybridized, Hence, the molecule has tetrahedral shape.It is not planar

subject: Chemistry
solution: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>BeH</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is indeed considered to be less stable compared to other hydrides among the given due to its significant covalent character and electron deficiency. Beryllium, being a small and highly charged cation, attracts the electrons in the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Be</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math> bonds more strongly, resulting in a greater degree of electron sharing between beryllium and hydrogen. This covalent character is in contrast to other alkaline earth metal hydrides, which tend to have more ionic character.

solution: The difference between the zeolite and ion exchange process is that the zeolite process uses the mineral zeolite as the exchanging resin for cations in the hard water whereas the ion exchange process include several different resins for the ion exchange. Synthetic resins method is more efficient as it can exchange both cations and anions.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Hydrogen is the first element in the periodic table and has the atomic number one. Those elements which have the same atomic number but a different mass number are called isotopes. There are three isotopes of hydrogen namely, protium H11, deuterium H12 or D and lastly tritium H13 or T. The isotopes are different because of the different number of neutrons present in them.

Isotopes of Hydrogen:



Among all three isotopes only Tritium is radioactive.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ acts as a reducing agent only in presence of strong oxidising agents (i.e., $\left.\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}\right)$in acidic as well as alkaline medium.
$$
\begin{aligned}
&2 \mathrm{KMnO}_4+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4+5 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow \\
&\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4+2 \mathrm{MnSO}_4+8 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+5 \mathrm{O}_2
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
For kinetic study of the reaction of iodide ion with H2O2 at room temperature following Precautions needs to be used.

  • Always keep the concentration of sodium thiosulphate solution less than that of potassium iodide solution.
  • Always use freshly prepared starch solution.
  • Use fresh samples of hydrogen peroxide and potassium iodide.
  • Always use the same measuring cylinders for measuring solutions in two different sets of observations. If after measuring one solution, the cylinder is used for measuring another solution, clean it before using.
  • Record the time immediately after the appearance of blue colour.

So, among the given statements only the statement A, B, and C are correct.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Molarity of H2O=Volume strength11.2

M=11.211.2=1

∴ 1 molar means 1000 mL solution contains 1 mol H2O2=1×34 g

Since, the strength is represented as weight of the solute in 1 L of the solution. The strength of the solution will be 34 g L-1.

Note: The question should've mentioned that we need to find the mass by volume percentage.

Percentage w/v =341000×100=3.4%

solution:
Cs+H2Og⟶1270 K T1COg+H2g is known as coal gasification reaction. The mixture produced in this reaction is called water gas.

COg+H2Og673 K T2Catalyst→CO2g+H2g is known as water gas shift reaction. Using water gas shift reaction we can increase the concentration of hydrogen gas.

Hence, T1>T2

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In basic medium, oxidising action of H2O2.

Mn2++H2O2→Mn+4+2OH-

In basic medium, reducing action of H2O2

I2+H2O2+2OH-→2I-+2H2O+O2

In acidic medium, oxidising action of H2O2

PbS(s)+4H2O2(aq)→PbSO4( s)+4H2O(l)

Hence correct option (4).

subject: Chemistry
options: reaction of zinc with aqueous alkali.
electrolysis of warm aqueous barium hydroxide solution between nickel electrodes.
electrolysis of acidified water using platinum electrodes.
reaction of zinc with dilute acid.
solution:
Dihydrogen of high purity (>99.95%) is obtained by electrolysing warm aqueous barium hydroxide solution between nickel electrodes. In electrolysis, the reaction occurs at the anode and cathode :-

At anode :- 2OH-→H2O + 12O2 + 2e-

At cathode :- 2H2O + 2e- →2OH- +H2

Hence, the high purity dihydrogen is obtained by electrolysing warm aqueous barium hydroxide.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Here, the H2O2 act as the reducing agent, as the Cl has +1 oxidation state of in NaOCl and after reaction with the H2O2, in the presence of the acid, it reduces the Cl in NaOCl to Cl- having -1 oxidation state.

So, as Cl oxidation state decreases from the +1 to -1 oxidation state, hence the H2O2 is acting as the reducing agent.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Heavy water is $\mathrm{D}_2 \mathrm{O}$ hence
number of electrons $=2+8=10$
number of protons $=10$
Atomic mass of $\mathrm{D}_2 \mathrm{O}=4+16=20$
hence number of neutron
$=$ Atomic mass $-$ number of protons
$$
=20-10=10
$$
